summary:
Endothelial cells primarily produce von Willebrand factor within their Weibel-Palade bodies; while megakaryocytes also play a role laterally, particularly during platelet release reactions, endothelial cell production remains central.
```

The summary focuses clearly on two main aspects—primary producer identification and secondary contributor involvement—to directly address what was asked about the source of secretion. ```plaintext Summary: 
Endothelial cells mainly secrete von Willebrand factor using Weibel-Palade bodies, although megakaryocytes store and release significant amounts post-activation. The primary responsibility lies with endothelial cells due to continuous initial synthesis.
```
